response:
We decided to stop scoping SyncEventGrid by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service‑blocks alone. Instead:

- Scope to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations.
- Require lighthouse_delegations.managed_by_tenant_id == the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in (active, succeeded).
- Apply Rackspace ownership filtering (only include subscriptions actually delegated to the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant).
- The sync still runs using the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential.

The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and inte", "2026-04-09: For azr-compliance-v3 PR #72 on 2026-04-09, two live GitHub review threads on GetEventGridDeployments were valid and fixed in commit 633573dc on develop. Fixes: (1) summary query no longer prepends an extra WITH before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE, preventing invalid SQL 'WITH WITH ...'; (2) selected tenant_id now uses COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id) to match join/filter fallback behavior. Added unit assertions to catch both regressions, reran ruff and focused pytest, rep", "2026-04-09: Implemented dedicated server-side Event Grid bulk remediation in azr-compliance-v3. Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/cus", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, three additional Event Grid review findings were validated and fixed in commit 5cd48c3. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding ", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, Event Grid eligibility was tightened so Event Grid subscriptions are only created/maintained for monitoring-entitled service blocks. Changes were made locally in source_data, eventgrid activity/orchestrators, onboarding, validation, http_eventgrid, and OpenAPI docs. Customer-wide Event Grid orchestration now uses only Lighthouse subscriptions with computed_service_tier='advanced'; ensure_event_subscription_single now skips non-lighthouse or non-monitoring sub", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27 in fleet-platform-app, investigated why global Event Grid filters were not excluding Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces in prod. Canonical Event Grid subscription name remains rax-resource-change-{customer_id[:12]}. Added shared classification utilities for live subscription-scoped Event Grid resources in shared/eventgrid_utils.py, including extraction of resource name, webhook URL, delivery attributes, and classification into canonical / legacy Fleet-targeting / external.
